name: Miniature Cannoli
recipeInstructions:
Special equipment: pastry bag with star tip.
Cut 30 rectangles measuring 3-inch by 12-inches from heavy duty aluminum foil. Fold the rectangles to create a cylinder with dimensions of 3/4 inch in diameter and 6-inches long. These foil cylinders will serve as molds for the miniature cannoli and can be reused.
Unroll the refrigerated dough and cut each into strips that are 1/2-inch wide. Brush one end of each strip with melted butter, then spiral it around the foil cylinder, starting and ending about 1/4 inch from the top and bottom edges. Ensure the spirals fit snugly together.
Place the mini cannoli on an ungreased baking sheet, spacing them 2-inches apart. Bake according to package instructions.
While the cannoli are baking, mix Brie cheese, room temperature butter, and dry white wine until well combined. Transfer the mixture to a star-tipped pastry bag.
Allow the cannoli to cool completely, then remove the foil cylinders. Pipe the cheese-butter filling into each cannoli using the pastry bag and serve. Makes 30 mini cannoli.
Do-Ahead Note: Prepare the cannoli rolls up to several days in advance and freeze them in a moisture-proof container. When ready to use, warm the molds slightly in an oven and fill with the cheese-butter mixture. The filling is best prepared on the day of serving.
